biblical.framework: The Son is Homoousios (of the same substance)
biblical.evidence: Jesus is fully God and eternal. Athanasius argued that only a fully divine Savior could reconcile humanity to God.

conventional.framework: The Son is a created being
conventional.evidence: Arius taught 'there was a time when the Son was not,' seeking to preserve Greek concepts of God's absolute indivisible oneness.

comparisons.resolution: The Council of Nicaea condemned Arianism, affirming the biblical truth of the Trinity and securing the foundation of the Christian Gospel.

comparisons.topic: Survival vs. Extinction under Islamic Rule

biblical.evidence: The Bible and liturgy were translated into Coptic (the indigenous language), giving the faith deep roots among the common people despite dhimmi status.

conventional.evidence: The church remained tied to Latin and Roman culture; the failure to translate the Bible into Berber meant the faith collapsed when Roman structures fell.

comparisons.resolution: This historical contrast vividly demonstrates the absolute necessity of Bible translation and cultural contextualization for the survival and flourishing of the church.
